document.title: Medical devices – Part 1: Application of usability engineering to medical devices

sourceSummary.oneLine: IEC 62366-1 Edition 1.1 specifies a usability engineering process for medical device usability as it relates to safety.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used by manufacturers to analyse, specify, develop and evaluate medical device usability related to safety, and to support conformity assessment against safety and performance essential principles.
keyPoints: The standard applies to usability engineering for medical devices as it relates to safety and normal use, including correct use and use error.
It can be used to identify risks associated with abnormal use but does not assess or mitigate those risks.
Manufacturers must establish, document, implement and maintain a usability engineering process and store results in a usability engineering file.

Risk control related to user interface design follows ISO 14971:2019 priorities: inherently safe design and manufacture, protective measures, and information for safety and training where appropriate.

The process includes use specification, identifying user interface safety characteristics and use errors, identifying hazards and hazardous situations, hazard-related use scenarios, user interface specification, evaluation planning, formative evaluation and summative evaluation.
Annex C provides an alternative process for user interfaces of unknown provenance.

entities.name: International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC)
entities.evidence: The International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC) is a worldwide organization for standardization comprising all national electrotechnical committees.

entities.name: IEC subcommittee 62A
entities.evidence: International Standard IEC 62366-1 has been prepared by a joint working group of subcommittee 62A: Common aspects of electrical medical equipment used in medical practice

entities.name: IEC technical committee 62
entities.evidence: of IEC technical committee 62: Electrical medical equipment in medical practice

entities.name: ISO technical committee 210
entities.evidence: and ISO technical committee 210: Quality management and corresponding general aspects for MEDICAL DEVICES.

entities.name: Manufacturers
entities.evidence: This part of IEC 62366 specifies a PROCESS for a MANUFACTURER to analyse, specify, develop and evaluate the USABILITY of a MEDICAL DEVICE as it relates to SAFETY.

entities.name: Medical devices
entities.evidence: Medical devices – Part 1: Application of usability engineering to medical devices

concepts.name: Usability engineering process
concepts.evidence: The MANUFACTURER shall establish, document, implement and maintain a USABILITY ENGINEERING PROCESS, as defined in Clause 5, to provide SAFETY for the PATIENT, USER and others.

concepts.name: Usability engineering file
concepts.evidence: The results of the USABILITY ENGINEERING PROCESS shall be stored in the USABILITY ENGINEERING FILE.

concepts.name: Use specification
concepts.evidence: The MANUFACTURER shall prepare a USE SPECIFICATION.

concepts.name: Use error
concepts.evidence: USER action or lack of USER action while using the MEDICAL DEVICE that leads to a different result than that intended by the MANUFACTURER or expected by the USER

concepts.name: Normal use
concepts.evidence: USE ERROR can occur in NORMAL USE.

concepts.name: Correct use
concepts.evidence: CORRECT USE: NORMAL USE without USE ERROR

concepts.name: Abnormal use
concepts.evidence: conscious, deliberate act or deliberate omission of an act that is counter to or violates NORMAL USE and is also beyond any further reasonable means of USER INTERFACE-related RISK CONTROL by the MANUFACTURER

concepts.name: Hazard-related use scenario
concepts.evidence: USE SCENARIO that could lead to a HAZARDOUS SITUATION or HARM

concepts.name: User interface specification
concepts.evidence: The MANUFACTURER shall establish and maintain a USER INTERFACE SPECIFICATION.

concepts.name: User interface evaluation plan
concepts.evidence: The MANUFACTURER shall establish and maintain a USER INTERFACE EVALUATION plan for the USER INTERFACE.

concepts.name: Formative evaluation
concepts.evidence: USER INTERFACE EVALUATION conducted with the intent to explore USER INTERFACE design strengths, weaknesses, and unanticipated USE ERRORS

concepts.name: Summative evaluation
concepts.evidence: USER INTERFACE EVALUATION conducted at the end of the USER INTERFACE development with the intent to obtain OBJECTIVE EVIDENCE that the USER INTERFACE can be used safely

concepts.name: User interface of unknown provenance
concepts.evidence: USER INTERFACE or part of a USER INTERFACE of a MEDICAL DEVICE previously developed for which adequate RECORDS of the USABILITY ENGINEERING PROCESS of this standard are not available

concepts.name: Information for safety
concepts.evidence: When, in accordance with the priorities of 4.1.2, information for SAFETY is used as a RISK CONTROL measure, the MANUFACTURER shall subject this information to the USABILITY ENGINEERING PROCESS

evidence.claim: IEC 62366-1 Edition 1.1 is a 2020-06 consolidated International Standard for medical device usability engineering.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 62366-1 Edition 1.1 2020-06 CONSOLIDATED VERSION INTERNATIONAL STANDARD Medical devices – Part 1: Application of usability engineering to medical devices

evidence.claim: The consolidated version contains the first edition, corrigendum and Amendment 1.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 62366-1 edition 1.1 contains the first edition (2015-02) ... and its corrigendum (2016-07), and its amendment 1 (2020-06).

evidence.claim: IEC 62366-1 was prepared by IEC and ISO technical groups and published as a double logo standard.
evidence.evidenceText: International Standard IEC 62366-1 has been prepared by a joint working group ... and ISO technical committee 210 ... It is published as double logo standard.

evidence.claim: The standard specifies a process for manufacturers to analyse, specify, develop and evaluate medical device usability as it relates to safety.
evidence.evidenceText: This part of IEC 62366 specifies a PROCESS for a MANUFACTURER to analyse, specify, develop and evaluate the USABILITY of a MEDICAL DEVICE as it relates to SAFETY.

evidence.claim: The process permits assessment and mitigation of risks associated with normal use, including correct use and use error.
evidence.evidenceText: This USABILITY ENGINEERING ... PROCESS permits the MANUFACTURER to assess and mitigate RISKS associated with NORMAL USE, i.e., CORRECT USE and USE ERROR.

evidence.claim: The standard can identify but does not assess or mitigate risks associated with abnormal use.
evidence.evidenceText: It can be used to identify but does not assess or mitigate RISKS associated with ABNORMAL USE.

evidence.claim: If the usability engineering process is complied with, usability related to safety is presumed acceptable unless objective evidence shows otherwise.
evidence.evidenceText: If the USABILITY ENGINEERING PROCESS detailed in this International Standard has been complied with, then the USABILITY of a MEDICAL DEVICE as it relates to SAFETY is presumed to be acceptable, unless there is OBJECTIVE EVIDENCE to the contrary.

evidence.claim: ISO 14971:2019 is the normative reference for risk management in this standard.
evidence.evidenceText: ISO 14971:2019, Medical devices – Application of risk management to medical devices

evidence.claim: Manufacturers must establish, document, implement and maintain a usability engineering process.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER shall establish, document, implement and maintain a USABILITY ENGINEERING PROCESS, as defined in Clause 5, to provide SAFETY for the PATIENT, USER and others.

evidence.claim: Usability engineering activities must be performed by competent personnel.
evidence.evidenceText: USABILITY ENGINEERING activities for a MEDICAL DEVICE shall be planned, carried out, and documented by personnel competent on the basis of appropriate education, training, skills or experience.

evidence.claim: Risk control related to user interface design follows ISO 14971:2019 priorities.
evidence.evidenceText: To reduce use-related RISK, the MANUFACTURER shall use one or more of the following options, in the priority listed (as required by ISO 14971:2019, 7.1): a) inherently safe design and manufacture; b) protective measures ... c) information for SAFETY and, where appropriate, training to USERS.

evidence.claim: Information for safety used as a risk control must be assessed through the usability engineering process.
evidence.evidenceText: When ... information for SAFETY is used as a RISK CONTROL measure, the MANUFACTURER shall subject this information to the USABILITY ENGINEERING PROCESS

evidence.claim: The results of the usability engineering process must be stored in the usability engineering file.
evidence.evidenceText: The results of the USABILITY ENGINEERING PROCESS shall be stored in the USABILITY ENGINEERING FILE.

evidence.claim: A use specification must include intended medical indication, intended patient population, body part or tissue, intended user profile, intended use environment and operating principle.
evidence.evidenceText: The USE SPECIFICATION shall include: intended medical indication; intended PATIENT population; intended part of the body or type of tissue applied to or interacted with; intended USER PROFILE; intended USE ENVIRONMENT; and operating principle.

evidence.claim: Manufacturers must identify user interface characteristics related to safety and potential use errors.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER shall identify USER INTERFACE characteristics that could be related to SAFETY as part of a RISK ANALYSIS performed according to ISO 14971:2019, 5.3.

evidence.claim: Manufacturers must identify known or foreseeable hazards and hazardous situations related to use of the medical device.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER shall identify known or foreseeable HAZARDS and HAZARDOUS SITUATIONS, which could affect PATIENTS, USERS or others, related to use of the MEDICAL DEVICE.

evidence.claim: Hazard-related use scenarios must be identified and described with tasks, sequences and severity of associated harm.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER shall identify and describe the reasonably foreseeable HAZARD-RELATED USE SCENARIOS ... The description ... shall include all TASKS and their sequences as well as the SEVERITY of the associated HARM.

evidence.claim: Manufacturers must select hazard-related use scenarios for summative evaluation and document the selection scheme.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER shall select the HAZARD-RELATED USE SCENARIOS to be included in the SUMMATIVE EVALUATION ... A summary of any selection scheme, the rationale for its use and the results of applying it shall be stored in the USABILITY ENGINEERING FILE.

evidence.claim: The user interface specification must include testable technical requirements relevant to the user interface.
evidence.evidenceText: The USER INTERFACE SPECIFICATION shall include: testable technical requirements relevant to the USER INTERFACE, including the requirements for those parts of the USER INTERFACE associated with the selected RISK CONTROL measures.

evidence.claim: The user interface evaluation plan must document objectives and methods for planned formative and summative evaluations.
evidence.evidenceText: The USER INTERFACE EVALUATION plan shall document the objective and identify the method of any planned FORMATIVE EVALUATIONS and SUMMATIVE EVALUATIONS.

evidence.claim: Summative evaluation is performed on the final or production-equivalent user interface for each selected hazard-related use scenario.
evidence.evidenceText: Upon completion of the design and implementation of the USER INTERFACE, the MANUFACTURER shall perform a SUMMATIVE EVALUATION of each HAZARD-RELATED USE SCENARIO selected in 5.5 on the final or production equivalent USER INTERFACE according to the USER INTERFACE EVALUATION plan.

evidence.claim: UOUP may be evaluated according to Annex C instead of all requirements of 5.1 through 5.9.
evidence.evidenceText: Instead of all the requirements of 5.1 through 5.9, UOUP may be evaluated according to Annex C.

evidence.claim: Annex C requires review of post-production information for incidents or near incidents for medical devices with UOUP.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER of the MEDICAL DEVICE with UOUP shall review available POST-PRODUCTION information including complaints and field reports for incidents or near incidents.

evidence.claim: The document supports essential principles of safety and performance for non-IVD and IVD medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: This document has been prepared to support the essential principles of safety and performance of non-IVD MEDICAL DEVICES ... according to ISO 16142-1:2016 ... This document has been prepared to support the essential principles of safety and performance of IVD MEDICAL DEVICES ... according to ISO 16142-2:2017.
evidence.pageTarget: Essential Principles Of Safety And Performance

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the document text identifies IEC and ISO as the issuing standards organizations; FDA appears only in bibliography references.
Source text includes both redline and final versions and duplicate English/French content; extracted facts prefer the final English version where available.
No product-specific model names are present; productModels is empty.
Some OCR/preprocessing artifacts are present, including duplicated pages, spaced small-cap terms, and inconsistent inserted text from redline markup.
